tasks.description: URL capture fails on 3/4 platforms. Only X works. Without post URLs, metric scraping and billing cannot run.

tasks.description: slowapi not installed on server. Add rate limiting to login/register endpoints (5/minute).

tasks.title: Invitation UX â€” countdown timers, expired badge, decline reason
tasks.description: No countdown timers on invitations, no visual expired state, no decline reason capture.

tasks.title: Metrics accuracy â€” populate anomaly_flag, cross-validation
tasks.description: anomaly_flag column exists but never populated. No cross-validation between Playwright and API metrics.

tasks.title: [DEFERRED] Repost campaign â€” company creation, frequency, user display
tasks.description: Full repost campaign feature: companies provide exact pre-written post content per platform instead of AI generation. Formats: text only, image only, text+image. Posting frequency: once, daily, every X days, weekly (stored as repost_frequency on Campaign model). Company dashboard: create form with per-platform text + image URL fields, campaign detail shows repost content card with edit support (POST /campaigns/{id}/repost-content), frequency selector. User dashboard: repost drafts shown as read-only with banner, no edit/regenerate, approve/reject only. Background agent: skips 4-phase AI pipeline for reposts, uses CampaignPost records directly, respects frequency setting, converts Reddit title+body format. Bugs already fixed: field name mismatch (campaign_posts vs repost_content) in local_db.py and background_agent.py, Reddit format conversion. Quality gate: image-only posts valid, content_guidance not required. Content rotation (multiple posts per platform) is a future sub-feature.

tasks.title: Admin payout void/approve actions
tasks.description: Admin payout page is read-only. Need void and approve buttons for fraud management.

tasks.title: Metric scraping â€” detailed spec per platform, verify E2E
tasks.description: Write detailed spec for what each platform scraper extracts. Reddit views ARE available (visible on post pages) -- Playwright preferred over PRAW for Reddit since PRAW cannot get views. Per-platform: views on X + Reddit, likes all 4, comments all 4, reposts not Reddit. Verify against real posts.

tasks.title: Billing â€” detailed spec for earnings calc, verify E2E
tasks.description: Spec for billing pipeline: rate_per_1k_views applies to X AND Reddit (not X-only). CPM multiplier, budget cap, hold period, platform cut, tier promotion. Campaign wizard should note views are billable on Reddit. Verify with real metrics.

tasks.title: Earnings display â€” server-to-local sync, withdrawal, verify E2E
tasks.description: No sync from server Payout records to local_earning. User app shows $0. Spec sync mechanism, breakdown, withdrawal flow.

tasks.title: AI matching â€” detailed spec for scoring logic, verify accuracy
tasks.description: Spec for matching prompt, scoring criteria, brand safety, audience overlap. Verify with real campaigns and profiles.

tasks.title: AI profile scraping â€” Gemini Vision, per-platform detailed spec
tasks.description: Per-platform spec (X, LinkedIn, Facebook, Reddit) for all data to extract. Current implementation is single-screenshot, unverified.

tasks.title: 4-phase content agent â€” research, strategy, creation, review
tasks.description: Current strategy is static dict. Needs deeper spec for goal-driven strategy, creator voice matching, trending topics, platform-native content.

tasks.title: AI campaign quality gate â€” detailed rubric spec
tasks.description: Current rubric is static 8-rule scoring. Needs niche-specific thresholds, inter-field validation, actionable feedback.

tasks.title: Content formats â€” threads, polls, carousels across platforms
tasks.description: Research most-used formats per platform by engagement data. Build JSON posting scripts for top formats. Re-enable Instagram/TikTok.

tasks.title: Free/Pro tiers â€” wire Stripe subscription billing
tasks.description: SUBSCRIPTION_TIERS config exists. Wire Stripe subscription checkout, webhook handler, enforce feature gates.

tasks.description: Zero automated tests. Create pytest infrastructure, server/client/integration tests covering the full money loop.

tasks.description: Static site explaining Amplifier. CTAs for download (users) and create campaign (companies). Deploy to Vercel.

tasks.title: [DEFERRED] Local lightweight LLM for user-side AI operations
tasks.description: Post-launch. Replace cloud API calls with a lightweight local LLM on user's desktop/laptop for profile scraping, content generation, and other AI tasks. Eliminates API key dependency and cost.

tasks.title: E2E deleted post detection â€” full pipeline verification
tasks.description: Verify the full deleted post pipeline on ALL 4 platforms (X, LinkedIn, Facebook, Reddit): scraper visits deleted URL, detects deletion, marks post deleted locally, notifies server via PATCH /api/posts/{id}/status, server voids pending earnings, funds return to campaign budget. Must test each platform individually. Scraper detection was tested in Task #6 but the full server notification + earning voiding E2E flow was not.

tasks.title: [DEFERRED] UGC-style content â€” make AI content look authentic and drive viral views
tasks.description: Make AI-generated content look like authentic user-generated content that drives viral views. Content should not look AI-written or corporate. Posts must reference the company's actual product/service using latest trends, news, and product updates â€” this makes posts accurate and feel like real UGC from someone who actually uses the product. Includes: platform-native writing styles (casual X threads, story-format LinkedIn, community-tone Reddit), anti-AI detection (no AI buzzwords, varied sentence structure, imperfect human touches), engagement-optimized hooks (contrarian takes, curiosity gaps, emotional triggers tied to current events and product features), visual UGC aesthetic for images (phone-quality look, natural lighting, handwritten annotations vs polished graphics). Goal: content that gets shared because it feels real, not because it looks like an ad.

tasks.title: Fully disable X across Amplifier — hardcoded safety guard
tasks.description: X's anti-bot detection has suspended the user's accounts 3 times total (original X account permanently locked predating Amplifier; 2026-03-24 replacement account first block during E2E testing; 2026-04-14 replacement account suspended during test posting from Amplifier). config/platforms.json already has enabled=false but code paths still reference X. This task: (1) audit every X/twitter reference across scripts/, config/, server/, docs/, (2) ensure posting engine, profile scraping, metric scraping, content generation, matching, login_setup, and background agent ALL skip X unconditionally via a HARDCODED guard — not just the config flag, (3) surface the block in user-app UI with tooltip explaining the risk, (4) preserve code (don't delete) so we can re-enable later with a safe method (X API v2, stealth browser like camoufox, or equivalent). Acceptance: setting X enabled=true in config must NOT re-enable posting/scraping; safety guard in code must refuse. All X UI in settings shows disabled state with tooltip. Priority: trust-critical before user onboarding — a suspended user is a product-killer.

metadata.reason: safety-critical; 3 X suspensions total across 2 accounts

tasks.title: Plan + execute Vercel migration (target host undecided)
tasks.description: Server offline after Vercel billed ~$47/cycle (serverless model incompatible with Amplifier's high poll volume from user apps). Project deleted, billing disputed. Migration path NOT YET DECIDED — still in planning. Full planning doc with analysis, options, and step-by-step plan: docs/MIGRATION-FROM-VERCEL.md. Leading candidate is shared Hostinger KVM VPS (owned by Nili+Daniel, co-hosts TTE+Stock Buddy) but not confirmed. Fallback options include Render flat-fee and Railway.
tasks.details: READ FIRST: docs/MIGRATION-FROM-VERCEL.md — 12 sections covering options analysis, server requirements, step-by-step setup, env vars, client cutover, Vercel cleanup (~62 files), cutover sequence, rollback, cost comparison, follow-ups, and open questions. Before executing: (1) decide target host (Hostinger VPS vs Render vs Railway vs other), (2) confirm with Nili/Daniel if going Hostinger route — KVM tier, SSH access, existing Caddy/Redis/UFW, subdomain, (3) audit ENCRYPTION_KEY usage before rotating, (4) verify ARQ WorkerSettings entrypoint exists. Clients still pointing to dead Vercel URL via config/.env:7 and scripts/utils/server_client.py:22. Server is OFFLINE right now — users can't access anything until this ships.
